title: Alaskans ask Medicaid advisory group to rethink proposed limits on therapy
summary: A large crowd turned out Wednesday to the final public meeting of Alaska's Medicaid Reform Advisory Group, with the majority weighing in on proposed limits on therapy visits, which some said they need to maintain an independent life.

title: Laine Welch: Southeast groups push for protection from Canadian mines upriver
summary: When land and water rights pit one country against another, that’s when things really get tricky. That is the situation in Southeast Alaska, where residents find themselves downstream from several massive open pit gold and copper mines being developed in bordering British Columbia.

title: Alaska voters upset about public-shaming mailers, but experts say they work
summary: Some residents in Anchorage are outraged about an apparently new-to-Alaska, psychology-inspired get-out-the-vote mailing that threatens to share recipients' history of nonvoting with their friends and neighbors.

title: Judicial commission recommends suspension for Palmer judge over pay claims
summary: The Alaska Commission on Judicial Conduct has recommended a Palmer District Court judge be disciplined for improperly signing legal documents. In filings with the Alaska Supreme Court, the commission recommended a 45-day suspension without pay for William Estelle, who has served as a District Court judge since 2003. In two cases, Estelle violated judicial conduct by signing affidavits claiming payment for cases that he had not decided in a timely manner, the filing said. Judges are legally required to decide or complete cases within six months or their pay will be delayed until they are up to date. title: Gamergate: What it is and why it matters
summary: The hashtag #gamergate gets thrown around a lot on Twitter, YouTube, and press sites. What's at the heart of Gamergate?

title: Under fire, Kelly says FASD prevention group remains 'hesitant' to embrace free birth control
summary: Under fire for his remarks on birth control in this Daily News Q&A, Sen. Pete Kelly told Senate colleagues today that his FASD prevention think tank might one day endorse birth control as a prevention tool but is focused on free pregnancy tests for now.

title: She walks around house in the nude with curtains open
summary: My wife and I are comfortable walking around the house in the nude, but my boundaries are a little more strict than hers. For example, she thinks it's fine to walk around naked with the curtains open. It's not the front curtains, just the ones on the side, but we have neighbors on both sides.

title: Letter: Withdrawal will assist a good candidate
summary: My son starts school this fall, and the conversations I have had with other parents are deeply concerning. Few parents consider their neighborhood school their first (and best) option, and I want to fix that. I filed to run for School Board, but when the filing deadline passed, I found myself in a three-way race. One candidate supports vouchers  which I believe are an assault on public schools  and refused to sign the School Boards Code of Ethics. The other candidate, Kameron Perez-Verdia, is experienced, rational, civil and passionate about education.
